revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yakata Kofun Cluster | native_name =屋形古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|49|N|130|44|18|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|49|N|130|44|18|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakata Kofun Cluster'''|屋形古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Tominaga, Yoshii-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953, with the area under protection expanded in 1986 and in 2003.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139717|title=屋形古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakata Kofun Cluster consists of four ''[[decorated kofun]]'': The {{nihongo|'''Mezurashizuka Kofun'''|珍敷塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Torifunezuka Kofun'''|鳥船塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Furuhata Kofun'''|古畑古墳}}, and the {{nihongo|'''Hara Kofun'''|原古墳}}. These tumuli are built on an [[alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountain Range, at an elevation of 40 to 90 meters. All were {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round Kofuns: however only the Furuhata and Hara Kofun remain intact.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Mezurashizuka Kofun The Mezurashizuka Kofun, located at the northern end of the cluster, just south of the prefectural road Ukihagusano Kurume Line. It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a [[gondola]]-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two [[True toad|toads]].One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical [[haniwa]]. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Chikugo-Yoshii Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035103/index.html Ukiha city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/12688 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}}

It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a [[gondola]]-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two [[True toad|toads]].One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical [[haniwa]]. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Chikugo-Yoshii Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical [[haniwa]]. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Chikugo-Yoshii Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Yakata Kofun Cluster'''|屋形古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Tominaga, Yoshii-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953, with the area under protection expanded in 1986 and in 2003.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139717|title=屋形古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakata Kofun Cluster consists of four ''[[decorated kofun]]'': The {{nihongo|'''Mezurashizuka Kofun'''|珍敷塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Torifunezuka Kofun'''|鳥船塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Furuhata Kofun'''|古畑古墳}}, and the {{nihongo|'''Hara Kofun'''|原古墳}}. These tumuli are built on an [[alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountain Range, at an elevation of 40 to 90 meters. All were {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round Kofuns: however only the Furuhata and Hara Kofun remain intact.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Mezurashizuka Kofun The Mezurashizuka Kofun, located at the northern end of the cluster, just south of the prefectural road Ukihagusano Kurume Line. It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a [[gondola]]-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two [[True toad|toads]].One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical [[haniwa]]. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Chikugo-Yoshii Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical [[haniwa]]. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Chikugo-Yoshii Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

The {{nihongo|'''Yakata Kofun Cluster'''|屋形古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Tominaga, Yoshii-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953, with the area under protection expanded in 1986 and in 2003.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139717|title=屋形古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakata Kofun Cluster consists of four ''[[decorated kofun]]'': The {{nihongo|'''Mezurashizuka Kofun'''|珍敷塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Torifunezuka Kofun'''|鳥船塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Furuhata Kofun'''|古畑古墳}}, and the {{nihongo|'''Hara Kofun'''|原古墳}}. These tumuli are built on an [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountain Range, at an elevation of 40 to 90 meters. All were {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round Kofuns: however only the Furuhata and Hara Kofun remain intact.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Mezurashizuka Kofun The Mezurashizuka Kofun, located at the northern end of the cluster, just south of the prefectural road Ukihagusano Kurume Line. It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a [[gondola]]-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two [[True toad|toads]].One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical [[haniwa]]. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Chikugo-Yoshii Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Yakata Kofun Cluster'''|屋形古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Tominaga, Yoshii-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953, with the area under protection expanded in 1986 and in 2003.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139717|title=屋形古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakata Kofun Cluster consists of four ''[[decorated kofun]]'': The {{nihongo|'''Mezurashizuka Kofun'''|珍敷塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Torifunezuka Kofun'''|鳥船塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Furuhata Kofun'''|古畑古墳}}, and the {{nihongo|'''Hara Kofun'''|原古墳}}. These tumuli are built on an [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountain Range, at an elevation of 40 to 90 meters. All were {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round Kofuns: however only the Furuhata and Hara Kofun remain intact.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Mezurashizuka Kofun The Mezurashizuka Kofun, located at the northern end of the cluster, just south of the prefectural road Ukihagusano Kurume Line. It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[:en:bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a [[:en:gondola|gondola]]-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two [[:en:True toad|toads]].One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Chikugo-Yoshii Station|Chikugo-Yoshii Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyudai Main Line|Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035103/index.html Ukiha city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/12688 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakata Kofun Cluster]][[ja:屋形古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11465692]] Wikidata name: Yakata Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yakata Kofun Cluster | native_name =屋形古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|33|19|49|N|130|44|18|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|33.33027777777778|N|130.73833333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Ukiha|WD=Q850373}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|49|N|130|44|18|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakata Kofun Cluster'''|屋形古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Tominaga, Yoshii-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953, with the area under protection expanded in 1986 and in 2003.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139717|title=屋形古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakata Kofun Cluster consists of four ''[[decorated kofun]]'': The {{nihongo|'''Mezurashizuka Kofun'''|珍敷塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Torifunezuka Kofun'''|鳥船塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Furuhata Kofun'''|古畑古墳}}, and the {{nihongo|'''Hara Kofun'''|原古墳}}. These tumuli are built on an [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountain Range, at an elevation of 40 to 90 meters. All were {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round Kofuns: however only the Furuhata and Hara Kofun remain intact.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Mezurashizuka Kofun The Mezurashizuka Kofun, located at the northern end of the cluster, just south of the prefectural road Ukihagusano Kurume Line. It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[:en:bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a [[:en:gondola|gondola]]-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two [[:en:True toad|toads]].One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Chikugo-Yoshii Station|Chikugo-Yoshii Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyudai Main Line|Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[:en:bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a [[:en:gondola|gondola]]-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two [[:en:True toad|toads]].One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Chikugo-Yoshii Station|Chikugo-Yoshii Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyudai Main Line|Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

The {{nihongo|'''Yakata Kofun Cluster'''|屋形古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Tominaga, Yoshii-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953, with the area under protection expanded in 1986 and in 2003.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139717|title=屋形古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakata Kofun Cluster consists of four ''[[decorated kofun]]'': The {{nihongo|'''Mezurashizuka Kofun'''|珍敷塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Torifunezuka Kofun'''|鳥船塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Furuhata Kofun'''|古畑古墳}}, and the {{nihongo|'''Hara Kofun'''|原古墳}}. These tumuli are built on an [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountain Range, at an elevation of 40 to 90 meters. All were {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round Kofuns: however only the Furuhata and Hara Kofun remain intact.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Mezurashizuka Kofun The Mezurashizuka Kofun, located at the northern end of the cluster, just south of the prefectural road Ukihagusano Kurume Line. It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[:en:bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a [[:en:gondola|gondola]]-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two [[:en:True toad|toads]].One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Chikugo-Yoshii Station|Chikugo-Yoshii Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyudai Main Line|Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakata Kofun Cluster]][[ja:屋形古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11465692]] Wikidata name: Yakata Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yakata Kofun Cluster | native_name =屋形古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|33|19|49|N|130|44|18|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|33.33027777777778|N|130.73833333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Ukiha|WD=Q850373}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|49|N|130|44|18|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakata Kofun Cluster'''|屋形古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Tominaga, Yoshii-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953, with the area under protection expanded in 1986 and in 2003.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139717|title=屋形古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakata Kofun Cluster consists of four ''[[decorated kofun]]'': The {{nihongo|'''Mezurashizuka Kofun'''|珍敷塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Torifunezuka Kofun'''|鳥船塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Furuhata Kofun'''|古畑古墳}}, and the {{nihongo|'''Hara Kofun'''|原古墳}}. These tumuli are built on an [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountain Range, at an elevation of 40 to 90 meters. All were {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round Kofuns: however only the Furuhata and Hara Kofun remain intact.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Mezurashizuka Kofun The Mezurashizuka Kofun, located at the northern end of the cluster, just south of the prefectural road Ukihagusano Kurume Line. It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[:en:bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a [[:en:gondola|gondola]]-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two [[:en:True toad|toads]].One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Chikugo-Yoshii Station|Chikugo-Yoshii Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyudai Main Line|Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

A [[:en:bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a [[:en:gondola|gondola]]-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two [[:en:True toad|toads]].One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Chikugo-Yoshii Station|Chikugo-Yoshii Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyudai Main Line|Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Yakata Kofun Cluster'''|屋形古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Tominaga, Yoshii-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953, with the area under protection expanded in 1986 and in 2003.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139717|title=屋形古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakata Kofun Cluster consists of four ''[[decorated kofun]]'': The {{nihongo|'''Mezurashizuka Kofun'''|珍敷塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Torifunezuka Kofun'''|鳥船塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Furuhata Kofun'''|古畑古墳}}, and the {{nihongo|'''Hara Kofun'''|原古墳}}. These tumuli are built on an [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountain Range, at an elevation of 40 to 90 meters. All were {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round Kofuns: however only the Furuhata and Hara Kofun remain intact.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[:en:bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a [[:en:gondola|gondola]]-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two [[:en:True toad|toads]].One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Chikugo-Yoshii Station|Chikugo-Yoshii Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyudai Main Line|Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakata Kofun Cluster]][[ja:屋形古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakata Kofun Cluster]][[ja:屋形古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakata Kofun Cluster]][[ja:屋形古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11465692]] Wikidata name: Yakata Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yakata Kofun Cluster | native_name =屋形古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|33|19|49|N|130|44|18|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|33.33027777777778|N|130.73833333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Ukiha|WD=Q850373}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|49|N|130|44|18|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakata Kofun Cluster'''|屋形古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Tominaga, Yoshii-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953, with the area under protection expanded in 1986 and in 2003.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139717|title=屋形古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakata Kofun Cluster consists of four ''[[decorated kofun]]'': The {{nihongo|'''Mezurashizuka Kofun'''|珍敷塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Torifunezuka Kofun'''|鳥船塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Furuhata Kofun'''|古畑古墳}}, and the {{nihongo|'''Hara Kofun'''|原古墳}}. These tumuli are built on an [[:en:Alluvial fan|Alluvial fan]] at the northern foot of the Minou Mountain Range, at an elevation of 40 to 90 meters. All were {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round Kofuns: however only the Furuhata and Hara Kofun remain intact.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Mezurashizuka Kofun The Mezurashizuka Kofun, located at the northern end of the cluster, just south of the prefectural road Ukihagusano Kurume Line. It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[:en:bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a [[:en:gondola|gondola]]-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two [[:en:True toad|toads]].One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Chikugo-Yoshii Station|Chikugo-Yoshii Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyudai Main Line|Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035103/index.html Ukiha city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/12688 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}}

==Overview== The Yakata Kofun Cluster consists of four ''[[decorated kofun]]'': The {{nihongo|'''Mezurashizuka Kofun'''|珍敷塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Torifunezuka Kofun'''|鳥船塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Furuhata Kofun'''|古畑古墳}}, and the {{nihongo|'''Hara Kofun'''|原古墳}}. These tumuli are built on an {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan}} at the northern foot of the Minou Mountain Range, at an elevation of 40 to 90 meters. All were {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round Kofuns: however only the Furuhata and Hara Kofun remain intact.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Mezurashizuka Kofun The Mezurashizuka Kofun, located at the northern end of the cluster, just south of the prefectural road Ukihagusano Kurume Line. It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[:en:bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a {{ill|gondola|en|gondola|WD=Q182916|lt=gondola}}-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two {{ill|True toad|en|True toad|WD=Q55372|lt=toads}}.One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Chikugo-Yoshii Station|en|Chikugo-Yoshii Station|WD=Q4844938|lt=Chikugo-Yoshii Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} [[:en:Kyudai Main Line|Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The {{nihongo|'''Yakata Kofun Cluster'''|屋形古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Tominaga, Yoshii-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|en|Ukiha, Fukuoka|WD=Q850373|lt=Ukiha}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953, with the area under protection expanded in 1986 and in 2003.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139717|title=屋形古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakata Kofun Cluster consists of four ''[[decorated kofun]]'': The {{nihongo|'''Mezurashizuka Kofun'''|珍敷塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Torifunezuka Kofun'''|鳥船塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Furuhata Kofun'''|古畑古墳}}, and the {{nihongo|'''Hara Kofun'''|原古墳}}. These tumuli are built on an {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan}} at the northern foot of the Minou Mountain Range, at an elevation of 40 to 90 meters. All were {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round Kofuns: however only the Furuhata and Hara Kofun remain intact.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[:en:bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a {{ill|gondola|en|gondola|WD=Q182916|lt=gondola}}-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two {{ill|True toad|en|True toad|WD=Q55372|lt=toads}}.One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Chikugo-Yoshii Station|en|Chikugo-Yoshii Station|WD=Q4844938|lt=Chikugo-Yoshii Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} [[:en:Kyudai Main Line|Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakata Kofun Cluster]][[ja:屋形古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakata Kofun Cluster]][[ja:屋形古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakata Kofun Cluster]][[ja:屋形古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11465692]] Wikidata name: Yakata Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yakata Kofun Cluster | native_name =屋形古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|33|19|49|N|130|44|18|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|33.33027777777778|N|130.73833333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|en|Ukiha, Fukuoka|WD=Q850373|lt=Ukiha, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Ukiha|WD=Q850373}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|49|N|130|44|18|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakata Kofun Cluster'''|屋形古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Tominaga, Yoshii-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|en|Ukiha, Fukuoka|WD=Q850373|lt=Ukiha}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953, with the area under protection expanded in 1986 and in 2003.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139717|title=屋形古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakata Kofun Cluster consists of four ''[[decorated kofun]]'': The {{nihongo|'''Mezurashizuka Kofun'''|珍敷塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Torifunezuka Kofun'''|鳥船塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Furuhata Kofun'''|古畑古墳}}, and the {{nihongo|'''Hara Kofun'''|原古墳}}. These tumuli are built on an {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan}} at the northern foot of the Minou Mountain Range, at an elevation of 40 to 90 meters. All were {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round Kofuns: however only the Furuhata and Hara Kofun remain intact.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Mezurashizuka Kofun The Mezurashizuka Kofun, located at the northern end of the cluster, just south of the prefectural road Ukihagusano Kurume Line. It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[:en:bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a {{ill|gondola|en|gondola|WD=Q182916|lt=gondola}}-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two {{ill|True toad|en|True toad|WD=Q55372|lt=toads}}.One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Chikugo-Yoshii Station|en|Chikugo-Yoshii Station|WD=Q4844938|lt=Chikugo-Yoshii Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} [[:en:Kyudai Main Line|Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yakata Kofun Cluster | native_name =屋形古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|33|19|49|N|130|44|18|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|33.33027777777778|N|130.73833333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|en|Ukiha, Fukuoka|WD=Q850373|lt=Ukiha, Fukuoka|1=Ukiha, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Ukiha|WD=Q850373|1=Ukiha, Fukuoka}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|49|N|130|44|18|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakata Kofun Cluster'''|屋形古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Tominaga, Yoshii-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|en|Ukiha, Fukuoka|WD=Q850373|lt=Ukiha|1=Ukiha, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953, with the area under protection expanded in 1986 and in 2003.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139717|title=屋形古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakata Kofun Cluster consists of four ''[[decorated kofun]]'': The {{nihongo|'''Mezurashizuka Kofun'''|珍敷塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Torifunezuka Kofun'''|鳥船塚古墳}}, {{nihongo|'''Furuhata Kofun'''|古畑古墳}}, and the {{nihongo|'''Hara Kofun'''|原古墳}}. These tumuli are built on an {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan|1=Alluvial fan}} at the northern foot of the Minou Mountain Range, at an elevation of 40 to 90 meters. All were {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round Kofuns: however only the Furuhata and Hara Kofun remain intact.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Mezurashizuka Kofun The Mezurashizuka Kofun, located at the northern end of the cluster, just south of the prefectural road Ukihagusano Kurume Line. It was discovered in 1950, but it appears to have been demolished many years previous to this, and has only fragments of the back and right walls of its stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] remaining. The mural is located in a preservation facility and can be viewed by advance application to the Ukiha City Board of Education.The painting on the back wall uses red and blue pigments, and is composed of three colors that make use of the rock's surface. Four thick horizontal lines form a base, above which three quivers containing bows and arrows placed in the center. A [[:en:bracken fern|''warabi'' pattern]] is between the left and center quiver, and above the center quiver is a concentric circle pattern, and below is a {{ill|gondola|en|gondola|WD=Q182916|lt=gondola|1=Gondola}}-shaped boat with a sail. It also depicts a figure wearing a crown holding an oar, and there is a bird perched on its bow. Above and to the right of the quiver is a figure holding a shield or a bow, and below that are two {{ill|True toad|en|True toad|WD=Q55372|lt=toads|1=True toad}}.One of the characteristics of this ancient mural is the extensive use of dots in addition to lines. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Hara Kofun The Hara Kofun is located 110 meters south of the Mezurashizuka Kofun. It has a diameter of about 12.4 meters and a height of about 3.5 meters. The horizontal entry cave-style stone burial chamber with a total length of about 8.9 meters, and has red murals on the back stone and side walls. The mural on the back wall depicts a large ship with two large oars. It is manned by a figure operating an oar, along with a figure holding a bow, and a horse or a house containing a dead body. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Torifunezuka Kofun The Torifunezuka Kofun is located approximately 135 meters south of the Hara Kofun. The burial chamber has been destroyed, and only two steps of the hip stones on the back wall remain. On the first step of the back wall, there are depictions of large shields, concentric circular patterns, ships, and beads. The ship is a large vessel with large oars, a person is on board, and there are two masts for the sails. On the second row, there is a picture of a bird perched on the ship's bow.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Furuhata Kofun The Furuhata Kofun is located approximately 200 meters south of the Torifunezuka Kofun. It has a diameter of approximately 20 meters and a height of three meters. It was constructed in two stages, and lined with cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. The back wall of the stone burial chamber is decorated with concentric circular patterns, triangular patterns, and human figures in red.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Chikugo-Yoshii Station|en|Chikugo-Yoshii Station|WD=Q4844938|lt=Chikugo-Yoshii Station|1=Chikugo-Yoshii Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} [[:en:Kyudai Main Line|Kyudai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035103/index.html Ukiha city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/12688 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}}
